back_translation: The famine was severe in the land.
When they had finished eating the grain that they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, "Go back, buy us a little food."
Judah said to him, "The man solemnly warned us, saying, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.'
If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy food for you.
But if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.'"
Israel said, "Why have you brought trouble upon me by telling the man you had another brother?"

They said, "The man asked us in detail about ourselves and our kindred, saying, 'Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?' We told him according to these words. How could we know that he would say, 'Bring your brother down'?"
Judah said to Israel his father, "Send the boy with me, and we will rise and go, that we may live and not die — both we and you and our little ones.
I will be surety for him; from my hand you may require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, I will bear the blame forever.
If we had not delayed, surely by now we could have returned twice."

Their father Israel said to them, "If it must be so, then do this: take of the best of the land in your vessels and bring down to the man as a gift — a little balm and a little honey, spices and myrrh, pistachios and almonds.
Take double silver in your hand, and the silver that was returned in the mouth of your sacks return in your hand. Perhaps it was an oversight.
Take your brother also, and arise, return to the man.
May God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, that he may release to you your other brother and Benjamin. As for me, if I am bereaved, I am bereaved."
The men took this gift, and double silver they took in their hand, and Benjamin. They arose and went down to Egypt and stood before Joseph.
Joseph saw Benjamin with them and said to the steward of his house, "Bring the men into the house, and slaughter and prepare, for with me the men will dine at noon."
The man did as Joseph had said and brought the men into Joseph's house.

The men were afraid because they had been brought to Joseph's house, and they said, "Because of the silver returned in our sacks at the first, we have been brought in — to roll on us and fall upon us and take us as slaves with our donkeys."
They approached the man over Joseph's house and spoke to him at the entrance of the house.
They said, "Please, my lord, we did indeed come down at the first to buy food.
When we came to the lodging place and opened our sacks, behold, each man's silver was in the mouth of his sack — our silver in its weight. We have brought it back in our hand.
Other silver we have brought down in our hand to buy food. We do not know who put our silver in our sacks."
He said, "Peace to you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father gave you treasure in your sacks; your silver came to me." Then he brought Simeon out to them.
The man brought the men into Joseph's house, gave them water, and they washed their feet, and he gave fodder to their donkeys.
They prepared the gift for Joseph's coming at noon, for they had heard that they would eat bread there.
When Joseph came home, they brought him the gift that was in their hand into the house, and they bowed down to him to the ground.
He asked them concerning their welfare and said, "Is your old father well, of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?"
They said, "Your servant our father is well; he is still alive." And they bowed down and did obeisance.
He lifted up his eyes and saw Benjamin his brother, his mother's son, and said, "Is this your youngest brother of whom you spoke to me?" And he said, "God be gracious to you, my son."
Joseph hurried, for his compassion was kindled toward his brother, and he sought to weep. He entered his chamber and wept there.
He washed his face and came out, restraining himself, and said, "Set on bread."

They set for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians could not eat bread with the Hebrews, for it was an abomination to the Egyptians.
They sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth, and the men marveled one to another.
He took portions from before him to them, and Benjamin's portion was five times more than the portions of all of them. They drank and made merry with him.
